scaffold_note: STUB — author constructs a package instantiating an out-of-scope defeater (subjective model-form adequacy, human factors, organizational, etc.). Place the constructed package at specs/calibration/packages/<case_id>.jsonld and update package_path.
ground_truth_reasoning: Out-of-scope defeater: subjective model-form adequacy where domain expert judgment is the validation arbiter rather than structured V&V 40 evidence; the choice between competing model formulations rests on physician or biomechanics expert opinion that the methodology cannot capture in structured form. This defeater type falls outside the deliberate scope of the UofA catalog because it cannot be detected through structural analysis of the credibility evidence package. The methodology documents this category as out-of-scope in §2.9 with rationale that the defeater requires non-structural inputs the technical evidence cannot represent.

notes: OOS package constructed by Judge D under productive-OOS framing (Spec v1.6 §8.0). Package is structurally clean and passes SHACL on the V&V 40 profile; the embedded credibility argument depends on evidence not contained in the bundle. Author should review the methodology framing of each OOS package — the verdict itself is straightforward (OUT-OF-SCOPE) but the choice of which evidence-gap category each package demonstrates is a methodology-design call worth confirming.

Confirm_existing intent and target rule W-PROV-01 did not fire while baseline rules fired the standard pattern. Multiple verdict interpretations have merit: EXISTING-RULE-MISBEHAVIOR if the W-PROV-01 SPARQL pattern fails to detect a case it should catch, GENERATOR-ARTIFACT if the package fails to instantiate the defeater despite claiming to, REAL-GAP if the incomplete-knowledge defeater requires a different rule than W-PROV-01. scaffold_note: Judge-D-driven UNCERTAIN substitute (sampled 2026-05-05 with thinking_budget=4096 to recover thinking-mode anchoring used for the original cal-026..030 cases). Confidence 0.63.
ground_truth_reasoning: This case closely mirrors the canonical UNCERTAIN calibration anchor cal-028: Morrison COU, W-AR-01 target, jarzebowicz-wardzinski/argument_defeaters/D1-undermining, high subtlety, COV-WRONG outcome. The package has structurally present acceptance criteria URIs on all assessed factors, which means W-AR-01's structural predicate (detecting absence of acceptanceCriteria) would not fire. The high-subtlety D1-undermining defeater is arguably embedded in the 18%/±15% output-comparison discrepancy accepted without justification — a logical undermining of the argument's basis — but this pattern semantically overlaps with W-AR-02 rather than W-AR-01. Two competing explanations each have defensible support: (1) EXISTING-RULE-MISBEHAVIOR — W-AR-01 is too structurally coarse to detect a subtle, logically-grounded D1 defeater when criterion URIs are formally present; or (2) GENERATOR-ARTIFACT — the high-subtlety instruction caused the generator to drift away from a true no-acceptance-criteria instantiation, and the actual package lacks a genuine D1 pattern detectable by any plausible rule formulation. The package is SHACL-valid and well-formed, ruling out GEN-INVALID. No single verdict dominates on available evidence.
annotator: Judge D (Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6, thinking-mode budget=4096) per spec v1.6 §8.0
notes: 2026-05-05 substitution: replaces original cal-027-uncertain-undermining-d1 content. Original case (adv-2026-p2-014-w-si-02_low_morrison-cou1-v17) was Judge D hedging without thinking-mode; production judges all classified it as EXISTING-RULE-MISBEHAVIOR. New case is Judge D thinking-mode anchor: adv-2026-p2-001-w-ar-01_high_morrison-cou1-v05. Original Judge D reasoning preserved in audit log at dev/build/adversarial/phase3/uncertain_candidates_thinking/.

scaffold_note: Judge-D-driven UNCERTAIN substitute (sampled 2026-05-05 with thinking_budget=4096 to recover thinking-mode anchoring used for the original cal-026..030 cases). Confidence 0.55.
ground_truth_reasoning: This case presents the same fundamental ambiguity as the canonical UNCERTAIN anchor (cal-028): a Nagaraja COU1 package targeting W-AR-01 at high subtlety where the target rule did not fire. The package is well-formed (COV-WRONG, not GEN-INVALID), and every inspectable credibility factor carries a detailed, specific `acceptanceCriteria` field. This means the straightforward D1 pattern (literal absence of acceptance criteria) is NOT present on the surface. Two competing interpretations are both defensible. First, EXISTING-RULE-MISBEHAVIOR: the high-subtlety generator may have embedded D1-undermining in the argument structure (e.g., criteria exist but are not formally connected to the claim node, or criteria are tautological at the claim level), and W-AR-01's predicate logic requires a more literal absence that the subtle package avoids — the rule is too narrow. Second, GENERATOR-ARTIFACT: at high subtlety, the generator may have overshot and produced a package that does not actually instantiate D1-undermining at all; instead it incidentally instantiates D2-rebutting (W-AR-02 fires legitimately on the 'Accepted' decision with a below-required Test-conditions factor), and the D1 defeater the source taxonomy intended is simply not present in the package content. The 12,000-character truncation prevents inspection of the argument-structure nodes and the Test-conditions factor rationale, which would be the most diagnostic content for resolving this fork. Absent that, no single verdict dominates, and honest adjudication requires escalation.
